Title: Run a built-in patient summary report with valid parameters
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager with Reports privilege; at least one published report definition exists in the RefApp Reports module
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Reports app from the home screen | 2. Select the Patient Summary report from the list | 3. Enter the required parameters | 4. Click Run | 5. Wait for the report to render
Expected_Result: Report runs to completion, status shows Completed, and a result table with patient rows matching the criteria is displayed

Title: Verify report status transitions from Requested to Completed
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a long-running report definition is available
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger the report run | 2. Observe the status immediately after submit | 3. Poll the report status page until it changes | 4. Confirm the final status
Expected_Result: Status transitions Requested -> In Progress -> Completed without entering a Failed state, and the result becomes downloadable only after Completed

Title: Cancel an in-progress report run and verify state
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a report is currently In Progress
Test_Steps: 1. Start a long-running report | 2. While status is In Progress click Cancel | 3. Confirm the cancellation prompt | 4. Refresh the report queue
Expected_Result: Report status changes to Cancelled, no result file is produced, and the queue no longer shows it as active

Title: Run report with valid start and end date range
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report requires a date range parameter
Test_Steps: 1. Select a date-range report | 2. Enter a valid start date | 3. Enter a valid end date after the start date | 4. Click Run
Expected_Result: Report returns only records whose dates fall within the inclusive range and the parameter values are echoed in the report header

Title: Reject end date earlier than start date
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; date-range report selected
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a start date | 2. Enter an end date that precedes the start date | 3. Click Run
Expected_Result: A validation error is shown, the report does not run, and no result is generated

Title: Boundary single-day range start equals end
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; date-range report selected
Test_Steps: 1. Set start date and end date to the same calendar day | 2. Click Run
Expected_Result: Report runs and returns records timestamped within that single day inclusive of 00:00:00 to 23:59:59

Title: Reject malformed date input
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; date-range report selected
Test_Steps: 1. Type an invalid date string into the start date field | 2. Attempt to run the report
Expected_Result: Field-level validation rejects the input, report does not submit, and the user is prompted to correct the date

Title: Far-future and far-past boundary dates
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; date-range report selected
Test_Steps: 1. Set start date to 01-Jan-1900 | 2. Set end date to 31-Dec-2099 | 3. Click Run
Expected_Result: Report accepts the extreme but valid range and completes without overflow or crash, returning all matching records

Title: Run report filtered by a single location
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; multiple locations configured; report has a location parameter
Test_Steps: 1. Select a location-aware report | 2. Choose a single location from the dropdown | 3. Run the report
Expected_Result: Result contains only records associated with the selected location and excludes records from other locations

Title: Run report across multiple selected locations
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report supports multi-select location
Test_Steps: 1. Select a location-aware report | 2. Select two or more locations | 3. Run the report
Expected_Result: Result aggregates records from all selected locations and none from unselected locations

Title: Run report with no location selected when required
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; location parameter is mandatory
Test_Steps: 1. Select a report requiring a location | 2. Leave location unselected | 3. Click Run
Expected_Result: Validation prevents the run and prompts that location is required

Title: Pairwise combination of date range, location, and format
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report supports date range, location, and export format parameters
Test_Steps: 1. Select date range valid | 2. Select location Inpatient Ward | 3. Select format CSV | 4. Run | 5. Repeat with empty range, all locations, PDF | 6. Repeat with single-day, single location, Excel
Expected_Result: Each parameter combination produces a consistent and correct result without errors, validating interaction coverage

Title: Decision table for parameter validity combinations
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report with mandatory date range and optional location
Test_Steps: 1. Run with valid dates and location present | 2. Run with valid dates and location absent | 3. Run with invalid dates and location present | 4. Run with invalid dates and location absent
Expected_Result: Rules 1 and 2 succeed, Rules 3 and 4 are blocked with validation errors; outcomes match the documented decision table

Title: Export a completed report to CSV
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a completed report is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Open a completed report | 2. Click Export | 3. Select CSV format | 4. Save the file | 5. Open the CSV in a spreadsheet tool
Expected_Result: A well-formed CSV downloads with a header row, one row per record, and values matching the on-screen result

Title: Export a completed report to PDF
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a completed report is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Open a completed report | 2. Click Export | 3. Select PDF format | 4. Save and open the PDF
Expected_Result: A paginated PDF is produced with report title, run parameters, and tabular data legibly rendered

Title: Export a completed report to Excel
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a completed report is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Open a completed report | 2. Click Export | 3. Select Excel (xlsx) format | 4. Save and open the workbook
Expected_Result: An xlsx workbook downloads with correctly typed columns (dates as dates, numbers as numbers) and complete data

Title: CSV injection protection for formula-leading cell values
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a patient name field contains a formula-like value
Test_Steps: 1. Ensure a record has a value beginning with = or + or - or @ | 2. Export the report to CSV | 3. Open the CSV in a spreadsheet
Expected_Result: Leading formula characters are neutralized (prefixed/escaped) so no executable formula runs when opened, preventing CSV injection

Title: CSV preserves UTF-8 special characters and diacritics
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; records contain non-ASCII names
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report including patients with accented names | 2. Export to CSV with UTF-8 | 3. Open the file
Expected_Result: Special characters and diacritics render correctly without mojibake or data corruption

Title: Run report whose criteria match no records
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; criteria guaranteed to return zero rows
Test_Steps: 1. Select a report | 2. Enter a date range with no underlying data | 3. Run the report
Expected_Result: Report completes successfully and displays an explicit empty-state message rather than an error or blank screen

Title: Export an empty report to CSV
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a report returning zero rows is displayed
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report that returns no rows | 2. Export to CSV
Expected_Result: CSV contains only the header row with no data rows and opens without error

Title: Large-dataset readiness for high-volume report run
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; dataset of approximately 100000 records seeded in a performance environment
Test_Steps: 1. Select a high-volume report | 2. Set a broad date range covering all records | 3. Run the report | 4. Monitor render and memory
Expected_Result: Report completes within the agreed SLA, paginates results, and the application remains responsive without timeout or out-of-memory failure

Title: Streamed export of a large result set to CSV
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report returns a very large result set
Test_Steps: 1. Run a high-volume report | 2. Export to CSV | 3. Verify full row count in the downloaded file
Expected_Result: All rows export via streaming without truncation, the file row count equals the result count, and the browser does not crash

Title: Concurrent report runs do not exhaust resources
Preconditions: Performance environment available; multiple Data Manager sessions
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger several heavy reports concurrently from different sessions | 2. Observe queueing and completion | 3. Watch server resource utilization
Expected_Result: Reports are queued or processed without deadlock, each completes correctly, and no run corrupts another's output

Title: Schedule a report to run on a recurring cron
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager with scheduler privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open report scheduling | 2. Select a report | 3. Configure a recurring schedule | 4. Set parameters and recipients | 5. Save the schedule
Expected_Result: Schedule is saved and listed with the next run time, and the report executes automatically at the scheduled time

Title: Scheduled report failure triggers alert and retry
Preconditions: A scheduled report is configured; an induced failure condition exists
Test_Steps: 1. Configure a schedule referencing an invalid data source | 2. Wait for the scheduled run | 3. Inspect the run history and notifications
Expected_Result: Failed run is recorded with an error status, an alert is sent to the configured recipient, and retry policy behaves as configured

Title: Disable an active scheduled report
Preconditions: An active recurring schedule exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the schedule list | 2. Toggle the schedule to disabled | 3. Wait past the next scheduled time
Expected_Result: Schedule moves to Disabled state, no run occurs at the next scheduled time, and it can be re-enabled later

Title: Scheduled report respects timezone and DST boundary
Preconditions: Scheduler configured with a specific timezone observing DST
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule a report for a time crossing the DST spring-forward boundary | 2. Observe actual execution time
Expected_Result: Report executes at the correct wall-clock time accounting for the DST shift without double-running or skipping silently

Title: Audit log captures PHI record creation
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in as Registration Clerk
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new patient record | 2. Note the timestamp and acting user | 3. Open the audit log viewer | 4. Locate the create entry
Expected_Result: An audit entry records the create action with actor user, timestamp, patient identifier, action type CREATE, and source IP

Title: Audit log captures read access to PHI
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in as Clinician
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient dashboard to view PHI | 2. Open the audit log viewer | 3. Filter by the patient and action READ
Expected_Result: A READ/ACCESS audit entry is recorded identifying who viewed which patient and when, supporting who-accessed-what queries

Title: Audit log captures PHI record update with before and after
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in as Clinician with edit rights
Test_Steps: 1. Edit a patient demographic field | 2. Save the change | 3. Open the audit log | 4. Inspect the update entry detail
Test_Data: Field=Address; Old=10 Old St; New=20 New Ave
Expected_Result: An UPDATE audit entry records actor, timestamp, field changed, and the prior and new values for traceability

Title: Audit log captures PHI record deletion or void
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in as user with delete/void rights
Test_Steps: 1. Void or delete a patient observation | 2. Open the audit log | 3. Locate the delete/void entry
Expected_Result: A DELETE/VOID audit entry records actor, timestamp, target record, and the void reason

Title: Decision table mapping CRUD actions to audit fields
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; user able to perform all CRUD operations
Test_Steps: 1. Perform a CREATE and check captured fields | 2. Perform a READ and check fields | 3. Perform an UPDATE and check fields | 4. Perform a DELETE and check fields
Expected_Result: Each action produces an audit entry containing the mandatory fields (actor, timestamp, action, target, outcome) per the decision table

Title: Audit log entries cannot be edited via UI
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in as Administrator
Test_Steps: 1. Open an existing audit entry | 2. Attempt to modify any field via the UI | 3. Attempt to delete the entry
Expected_Result: No edit or delete control is available for audit entries, and any direct attempt is denied, preserving immutability

Title: Direct database update of audit row is blocked or detected
Preconditions: Database access available in a test environment; audit table populated
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt an UPDATE statement against an audit log row | 2. Attempt a DELETE statement | 3. Inspect constraints/triggers
Test_Data: SQL=UPDATE audit_log SET action='READ' WHERE id=...
Expected_Result: Database constraints, triggers, or append-only policy prevent or flag the modification, and tamper is detectable

Title: Hash-chain tamper-evidence detects altered audit record
Preconditions: Tamper-evident audit chain enabled; sequence of audit records exists
Test_Steps: 1. Compute the chain hash baseline | 2. Alter a single historical audit record at the storage layer | 3. Re-run chain verification
Expected_Result: Chain verification fails at the altered record, identifying the break point and proving tamper-evidence

Title: Sequential audit IDs detect gaps from deleted entries
Preconditions: Append-only audit store with sequential identifiers
Test_Steps: 1. Capture the current max audit sequence | 2. Remove a middle record at storage layer | 3. Run a gap-detection report
Expected_Result: Gap-detection report flags the missing sequence number, surfacing potential deletion of audit history

Title: Who-accessed-what report for a single patient
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; multiple users have accessed a patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open the access report | 2. Filter by a patient identifier | 3. Run the report
Expected_Result: Report lists every user who accessed the patient, the action, and the timestamp, supporting HIPAA accounting of disclosures

Title: Detect potential snooping by same-name access
Preconditions: Audit data includes staff accessing a patient sharing their surname
Test_Steps: 1. Run the access report | 2. Cross-reference accessor surname with patient surname | 3. Flag suspicious matches
Expected_Result: Suspicious self/same-name access is surfaced for privacy review, demonstrating break-the-glass monitoring capability

Title: Successful login is recorded in the audit log
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; valid user credentials available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in with valid credentials | 2. Open the security/audit log | 3. Locate the login entry
Expected_Result: Audit records a successful login with username, timestamp, source IP, and session identifier

Title: Failed login attempts are recorded
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a valid username with a wrong password | 2. Repeat the failed attempt several times | 3. Open the audit log
Expected_Result: Each failed login is audited with username, timestamp, and failure reason, enabling brute-force detection

Title: Logout and session expiry are recorded
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; an active session exists
Test_Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Explicitly log out | 3. In a separate session let it expire by timeout | 4. Inspect the audit log
Expected_Result: Both explicit logout and session timeout produce distinct audit entries with timestamp and session reference

Title: Account lockout after repeated failed logins is audited
Preconditions: Lockout policy enabled; account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Exceed the configured failed-login threshold | 2. Observe the lockout | 3. Inspect the audit log
Expected_Result: An account-lockout audit entry is recorded and subsequent attempts during lockout are also logged

Title: Unauthorized role cannot run restricted reports
Preconditions: Logged in as a low-privilege user without Reports privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open the Reports app | 2. Attempt to run a restricted report by direct URL
Expected_Result: Access is denied with an authorization error, the report does not run, and the denied attempt is audited

Title: Audit log viewer restricted to authorized roles
Preconditions: Logged in as a clinical user without audit-view privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to open the audit log viewer | 2. Attempt direct API access to audit endpoints
Expected_Result: Audit viewer is inaccessible, API returns 403, and the unauthorized attempt itself is logged

Title: Run a report via REST API with valid token
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid OAuth/session token for Data Manager
Test_Steps: 1. POST a report run request to the reporting REST endpoint | 2. Poll the status endpoint | 3. GET the result
Expected_Result: API returns 201/200 with a run UUID, status transitions to completed, and the result payload matches UI output

Title: Reject report run via API with invalid token
Preconditions: REST API enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a report run request with a missing or expired token | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized, no report runs, and the unauthorized API attempt is audited

Title: API rejects malformed parameter payload
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST a report run with a malformed JSON body | 2. POST with an out-of-range date parameter | 3. Inspect responses
Expected_Result: API returns 400 Bad Request with a descriptive validation error and does not execute the report

Title: Audit entry maps to FHIR AuditEvent resource
Preconditions: FHIR module/adapter enabled; an auditable action occurred
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a PHI read | 2. Retrieve the corresponding FHIR AuditEvent | 3. Validate the resource structure
Expected_Result: AuditEvent resource includes type, action, recorded timestamp, agent (who), source, and entity (what) per FHIR R4 with valid code system URIs

Title: FHIR AuditEvent action codes use correct value set
Preconditions: FHIR adapter enabled; create, read, update, delete events generated
Test_Steps: 1. Generate one AuditEvent per CRUD action | 2. Inspect the action element of each
Expected_Result: Action codes map correctly to FHIR AuditEvent action codes (C,R,U,D) and use the canonical code system URI

Title: Export report results as a FHIR Bundle
Preconditions: FHIR adapter enabled; report producing patient resources
Test_Steps: 1. Run a patient-list report | 2. Request FHIR Bundle export | 3. Validate the Bundle
Expected_Result: A valid FHIR searchset Bundle is returned with correctly typed entries and resolvable references

Title: Audit/data export conforms to HL7 v2 message structure
Preconditions: HL7 interface/adapter configured on the platform
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an HL7 export of an audit/ADT event | 2. Capture the outbound message | 3. Validate segments and encoding
Expected_Result: Message contains correctly ordered required segments (MSH, EVN, PID) with valid field separators and encoding characters

Title: Malformed HL7 audit message is rejected and logged
Preconditions: HL7 interface configured; ability to inject a malformed message
Test_Steps: 1. Send an HL7 message missing the MSH segment | 2. Observe interface handling
Expected_Result: The interface rejects the malformed message with an NAK/error and records the rejection in the audit/error log

Title: Report results table is screen-reader accessible
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; a completed report is displayed; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the report table using only the keyboard | 2. Use a screen reader to read headers and cells | 3. Check focus order
Expected_Result: Table has proper header associations, cells are announced with context, focus order is logical, and all controls are keyboard reachable per WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Export controls meet contrast and label requirements
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report toolbar visible
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect export buttons for accessible names | 2. Measure color contrast of controls and status text | 3. Verify error messages are announced
Expected_Result: Controls expose accessible names, contrast ratio meets at least 4.5:1 for text, and status/errors are programmatically announced

Title: Audit log retention policy enforces minimum period
Preconditions: Retention policy configured; audit records spanning several years
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect retention configuration | 2. Verify records older than the minimum are retained | 3. Verify archival of aged records
Expected_Result: Audit records are retained for at least the regulatory minimum (e.g., 6 years for HIPAA) and aged records are archived not silently purged

Title: Audit query uses index and avoids full scan
Preconditions: Audit table populated with large volume; DB query plan accessible
Test_Steps: 1. Run a who-accessed-what query filtered by patient and date | 2. Capture the execution plan
Expected_Result: Query plan uses appropriate indexes, avoids full table scans, and returns within the performance budget

Title: Audit timestamps use server time and are timezone-consistent
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; clients in different timezones
Test_Steps: 1. Perform auditable actions from clients in different timezones | 2. Compare recorded timestamps | 3. Verify timezone normalization
Expected_Result: All audit timestamps are stored in a consistent reference (e.g., UTC) preventing ambiguity and ordering errors

Title: Export masks restricted PHI per user authorization
Preconditions: Logged in as a user with limited PHI authorization; report includes sensitive fields
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report containing restricted fields (e.g., HIV status) | 2. Export to CSV | 3. Inspect masked fields
Expected_Result: Restricted PHI is masked or omitted in the export according to the user's authorization, and the export action is audited

Title: Exploratory session on report parameter edge interactions
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; access to multiple report definitions
Test_Steps: 1. Try unusual parameter orders | 2. Switch reports mid-configuration | 3. Use browser back/forward during a run | 4. Resubmit rapidly
Expected_Result: No crashes, stale parameters, or duplicate runs occur; the system handles unexpected navigation gracefully

Title: Audit captures bulk export as an access event
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; user exports a multi-patient report
Test_Steps: 1. Run a report covering many patients | 2. Export the results | 3. Inspect the audit log for the export
Expected_Result: A single audit entry (or per-patient entries) records the bulk export as a PHI access event with actor, count, and timestamp

Title: Maximum date range span boundary handling
Preconditions: Logged in as Data Manager; report enforces a max range span
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a range exceeding the configured maximum span | 2. Run | 3. Enter a range exactly at the maximum | 4. Run
Test_Data: MaxSpan=365 days; Range1=400 days; Range2=365 days
Expected_Result: Range exceeding the maximum is rejected with a clear message; the exactly-at-maximum range is accepted

Title: Pairwise of schedule frequency, format, and delivery channel
Preconditions: Scheduler and delivery channels configured
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule daily + CSV + email | 2. Schedule weekly + PDF + file share | 3. Schedule monthly + Excel + secure link
Expected_Result: Each frequency/format/channel pairing executes and delivers correctly, validating interaction coverage of scheduling options

Title: Audit write succeeds even when business transaction rolls back
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; ability to force a transaction rollback
Test_Steps: 1. Begin a PHI update that will fail and roll back | 2. Confirm the business change is reverted | 3. Inspect the audit log for the attempt
Expected_Result: The attempted/failed action is still audited (audit not lost to rollback), capturing the access attempt and its outcome
